name: Hurr ibn Yazid Al-Riyahi
title: The Returned Freeman
role: Former Enemy Commander, Martyr for Truth
shortDescription: A brave commander of Kufa who intercepted the Imam's caravan, but made a monumental choice at the last moment to repent and be martyred.
fullStory:
Hurr ibn Yazid al-Riyahi was a high-ranking commander, a nobleman, and a renowned and brave warrior of Kufa who held a very high position within his tribe. When Ibn Ziyad learned of Imam Hussain's (AS) movement toward Kufa, he dispatched Hurr on an urgent mission with an army consisting of a thousand cavalrymen to block the path of the Prophet's grandson's caravan and force them to surrender or stop.

Hurr's army encountered the Imam's caravan midway while they were severely exhausted from extreme heat and thirst. With unparalleled magnanimity and generosity, Imam Hussain (AS) ordered his followers to provide water not only to Hurr's thirsty soldiers but even to their horses. Despite this, bound by his military mission, Hurr accompanied the Imam's caravan and prevented them from moving toward Kufa or returning to Medina, ultimately forcing them to stop in the barren land of Karbala.

According to the precise and documented narrative of the book Al-Irshad written by Sheikh Mufid, on the morning of Ashura, when Hurr ibn Yazid realized that Umar ibn Sa'd definitively intended to engage in a bloody battle with the Prophet's grandson and would reject any of the Imam's peace proposals, his body began to tremble. One of his companions asked him in surprise, saying he was the bravest man in Kufa, so what was this trembling for? Hurr replied that he saw himself choosing between heaven and hell, and by God, he would prefer nothing over heaven, even if he were cut to pieces and burned.

According to Sheikh Mufid's report in Al-Irshad, Hurr then spurred his horse and moved toward Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan with his hands placed on his head as a sign of remorse, saying: 'O Lord, I have returned to You, so accept my repentance, for I made the hearts of Your friends and the children of Your Prophet tremble'. He came to the Imam and, with great shame, said: 'I am the one who kept you from returning and made things difficult for you; I did not think these people would take matters this far. Is my repentance accepted?'

Imam Hussain (AS) with open arms and utmost kindness said: 'Yes, God accepts your repentance and forgives you'. Revitalized by the Imam's forgiveness, Hurr went to the battlefield with his permission and delivered a crushing speech against the Kufan army, reprimanding them for their betrayal. He then attacked the enemy ranks and, after a fierce and heroic battle where he killed many enemies, attained the supreme triumph of martyrdom, eternally etching his name as the symbol of a free man.
quoteArabic: اللهم إليك أنبت فتب عليّ، فقد أرعبت قلوب أوليائك وأولاد نبيك.
quote: O Lord! I have returned to You, accept my repentance; for I made the hearts of Your friends and the children of Your Prophet tremble.
